ground_truth.title: 50 Hotel Hacks & Tips for Solo Travelers Staying in Hotels - GRRRLTRAVELER
ground_truth.author: Christine Kaaloa
ground_truth.main_content:
Although solo travelers appreciate the pampering and the privacy their own hotel room, it's not uncommon to experience stress about hotel etiquette and finding an affordable hotel. To take the stress and questions out of your hotel bookings, I'm sharing my survival guide of hotel hacks and tips for solo travelers.

1. Book a hotel located in the city - For solo travelers, booking a hotel stay that is conveniently located near city attractions is ideal. Prices may be higher for location convenience, but you'll encounter less navigational stress, better nightlife and food options and busy, well-light street safety at night.

2. Research hotel reviews - Check hotel guest reviews. Happy to angry customers will share their opinions and gives insight into the facilities in ways you cannot tell from a photo.

3. Book a hotel conveniently located near to public transportation - Skirting around town is easier if you can step outside your hotel and hop onto a public vehicle.

4. If you are staying in a hotel you feel unsafe in, leave - Obviously, I would not book a hotel I do not feel safe in. But let's say you book a hotel whose security upon arrival is questionable, you are the only woman in what feels like a male dominant hotel or the hotel does not look the way it appeared on the website. Book your next night in another hotel.

5. Pack a safety doorstop - I take a safety doorstop with me for guesthouses. For most of the hotels that I've stayed at, using the chain lock has been enough. But if you are a female solo traveler, packing a safety door stop does not hurt.

6. Do not leave your valuables lying around in your room - I do not leave any valuables out, when I leave the room, because I do not want to make theft easy or tempting.

7. Don't bring any valuables you are not prepared to carry during sightseeing - If I'm staying at guesthouses or hostels, where I feel like the doors feel a little flimsy and easy to open or the hostel locker doesn't feel stable, I will take my valuables with me.

8. DO use your 'Do not disturb' sign - When I used to worked as traveling camera crew, I stayed in hotel rooms from days to a month. As I was responsible for a lot of expensive equipment in my room, I didn't want housekeeping to tamper, clean or organize my belongings.

9. If you don't want to leave a housecleaning tip every single day, DO THIS - I put out the 'Do Not Disturb' sign on my door to keep housekeeping from entering and cleaning my hotel room. It's partially my thriftiness of not wanting to leave tips every day.

10. Always do a once over of your room before checking out - Before checking out, I always go through all the closets, drawers and look under my bed, under the sheets and in the bed cracks to make sure I don't leave any personal items behind.

11. Avoid the hotel safe or leave a shoe in it - Personally, I do not like using the hotel safe. I can be forgetful when I'm in a rush. I don't like to put my belongings in spaces where they can be concealed and I can forget them.

12. Tipping Etiquette in U.S. Hotels - Tipping is a personal preference, based on budget, cultural etiquette and the existing beliefs of the country you are from. In the U.S., tips are expected.

13. Tipping Etiquette in hotels abroad - Asian countries typically do not believe in tipping. In countries like Japan, tipping can be considered offensive and rude.

14. Beware of resort fees and taxes included in the bill - Sometimes, you may book a hotel at one price and upon check-out, discover there's been extra charges to your bill. Resort fees and city taxes can be the culprits.

15. Book hotel with free cancellation - Did you know you can Book refundable hotel reservations in advance and cancel them up to the last minute for free?

16. Book hotels with free WiFi or use your eSIM - If you are a digital nomad who works while traveling, book a hotel with free WiFi.

17. Book hotels with free parking - Hotel parking can be expensive and some require valet to park. Make sure your hotel has free parking.

18. Hotels with free breakfasts rock - Book a hotel with a hotel that offers free breakfast.

19. Book a hotel with a mini fridge - I love hotel rooms with a mini fridge, because I love going grocery shopping and bringing back yogurts, snacks and drinks!

20. Book a room with a kitchen if you have a long stay - I'm a huge fan of local grocery shopping and if I have a long stay or work trip, I'll look to see if a hotel with a kitchen is a viable option for me.

21. Pack a power strip or multi-port plug - If you're traveling with multiple devices, a 6 inch power strip or multi-port plug can be a lifesaver.

22. Your hotel coffee maker makes a great cooker - You may not have a microwave in your room but you'll likely have a coffee maker.

23. Bring a 3-in-1 spork - A 3-in-1 spork is a utensil that combines a spoon, serrated edge for cutting and a fork.

24. Ask for a room that is not near an elevator, ice maker and emergency stairs - If you are a light sleeper, getting a hotel room next to busy or noisy traffic areas such as elevators, emergency stairs and ice makers will be annoying.

25. Use hotel points strategically - If you're a frequent traveler, consider signing up for a hotel loyalty program.

26. Check out Booking.com loyalty program - Booking.com has a loyalty program that gives you savings and perks like room upgrades and free breakfast.

27. Pack wipes to clean high contact areas - There are many high contact areas that house cleaning does not clean, such as television remote control, door handles, toilet handle, telephone.

28. Chuck the decorative pillow and bed cover - The decorative pillows and bed throws are not regularly cleaned, but often reused.

29. Pack a universal plug adapter - These days I'm surprised how many hotels have international plug outlets but not all hotels have gotten up to speed with that.

30. Ask for early check-in time - Although the hotel website says that check in is at a specific time, but your flight arrives earlier, kindly ask if you can get an early check-in.

31. Ask for a late check-out time - Similar to above I've had hotels allow me a later check-out time without a fee.

32. Ask to store your luggage in their luggage storage room - If you are not allowed to check-in to your room or you have a substantial amount of time before you leave and want to go sightseeing, ask the hotel to store your luggage.

33. Consider booking weekdays over weekends - Hotels are often cheaper on weekdays than weekends.

34. Book a hotel with free airport pickup - Hotels with free airport pickups and drop-off services are perfect for solo travelers.

35. Hotels near the airport can sometimes be inexpensive - Sometimes, you can find great deals on airport hotels because they are more often used by travelers with long layovers.

36. Request two room card keys - There is nothing more annoying than returning to your room after a long tired day of shopping only to find your room card has been de-magnetized.

37. Use your AAA discount - AAA's roadside assistance isn't the only awesome perk of your AAA travel membership but you can save anywhere from 5%-10% off your favorite hotels.

38. Download a white noise or sleep sounds app - For light sleepers or those who have difficulty sleeping in new places, download White Noise Lite or Sleep Sounds by Sleeping Pillow.

39. Steam out wrinkles with your hotel shower - Another hotel hack is to hang wrinkled clothes in the bathroom when you take a shower and the steam from your bath will loosen the wrinkles.

40. Pack a blackout mask - Many hotels have blackout curtains but sometimes it's still not enough.

41. Turn off the lights before you leave your room - Let's all do our part to reduce energy waste.

42. Have a hotel routine - Having a hotel routine gives you a formula for setting up your room for cleanliness and comfort.

43. Bring flip flops - Please don't walk barefoot on the rug.

44. Tidy your room before leaving - I know the house keepers have a job to do but it's a dirty job.

45. If you are not planning to spend time at a hotel pool, do not book a hotel with resort fees - A resort fee is an additional charge tacked onto your bill for the use of hotel amenities.

46. Ask for an upgraded room - It never hurts to ask for an upgraded room when you arrive.

47. Book off-peak travel - If you have flexibility in your travel schedule, booking a hotel during off-peak season can open more room upgrades.

48. Your hotel location can affect your nightly activities - A viewer once asked me what's a good time to be off the streets as a solo traveler?
with: Although solo travelers appreciate the pampering and the privacy their own hotel room, it's not uncommon to experience stress about hotel etiquette and finding an affordable hotel.
For solo travelers, booking a hotel stay that is conveniently located near city attractions is ideal.
I take a safety doorstop with me for guesthouses. For most of the hotels that I've stayed at, using the chain lock has been enough.
Check hotel guest reviews. Happy to angry customers will share their opinions and gives insight into the facilities in ways you cannot tell from a photo.
Book a hotel with free airport pickups and drop-off services are perfect for solo travelers.
